rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are valid AADL features or declared helpers in the same annex.
Use assign statements only for implementation-level bindings with existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Place AGREE contracts in the correct AADL scope: component type annexes for assumptions/guarantees, implementation annexes for bindings.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers not present in the model.
Ensure AGREE scalar type declarations use int, real, or bool; avoid references to undefined types.
Validate that guarantee expressions have matching assumed and actual types to prevent type mismatches.
Do not add new architecture elements when repairing AGREE diagnostics; only fix existing references or declarations.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Implementation-level bindings should be placed in component implementation scopes after subcomponents and connections.
Constants and equation declarations belong in component type annexes unless they are implementation-specific.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new subcomponents or ports in AGREE repairs; only resolve existing references.
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scope; use only assign for bindings.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE validator diagnostics for connection ends
Component type vs implementation scope distinctions for assumptions
